mainText:
Good morning. Here are your headlines for Friday 29 May 2026.

AI models and products. Google shipped Gemini 3.5 Flash, Composer 2.5, Grok Build, and Antigravity 2.0 during the densest release cluster of 2026.

UK personal finance. Trading 212 tops easy-access ISA rates at 4.76 percent.

Israeli politics. Netanyahu directs military to take over 70 percent of Gaza territory.

UK politics. Labour lost 1,100 seats and Reform UK gained 1,400 in May local elections.

Sleep and wearables. Fitbit Air launched May 26 at 99.99 dollars, first new Fitbit in four years with Gemini AI coach.

B2B SaaS and go-to-market. Geordie AI raises 30 million dollars Series A led by Balderton Capital for enterprise AI agent security.

Today's question: With 46 percent of B2B GTM leaders planning to increase AI sales tool investment this year, are you positioning your fractional advisory clients to be ahead of that curve, or are you still seeing them treat agentic platforms as optional rather than foundational to their 2026 go-to-market motion?
fullText:
Good morning. Here is your daily brief for Friday 29 May 2026.

🤖 AI models and products
- Gemini 3.5 Flash launched as frontier-level intelligence at 4x the speed of comparable models with pricing of $1.50 per million input tokens and 76.2% on Terminal-Bench 2.1.
- Cursor's Composer 2.5 Standard tier costs $0.50 input and $2.50 output per million tokens, representing a new sustainable pricing tier for frontier-class coding.
- Subquadratic's SubQ 1M-Preview launched May 5 with $29 million seed funding and a native 12 million token context window at roughly 1/5 the cost of frontier models on long-context tasks.

💷 UK personal finance
- Easy-access ISA rates have peaked at 4.76 percent AER with bonuses included, while fixed one-year and two-year ISAs reach 4.66 and 4.71 percent respectively.
- Nationwide offers the best two-year fixed mortgage at 4.40 percent with a £999 fee for homemovers and first-time buyers.
- The Bank of England base rate stands at 3.75 percent after the latest cut in December 2025, with market speculation suggesting potential hikes later this year due to Middle East energy disruptions.

🇮🇱 Israeli politics
- Legislative elections scheduled for 27 October 2026 with Netanyahu's right-wing bloc polling at 50 out of 120 Knesset seats while opposition parties receive 60.
- Bennett and Lapid seeking to broaden their coalition by inviting Gadi Eisenkot, former chief of staff of the Israel Defense Forces and leader of the Yashar Party.
- Coalition partners Itamar Ben-Gvir and Bezalel Smotrich pressured Netanyahu to withdraw his initial proposal to open the Rafah crossing in both directions fully.

🇬🇧 UK politics
- Zoë Garbett became the first directly-elected Green mayor of Hackney, a former Labour stronghold.
- Labour lost control of Lambeth and Lewisham to the Green Party.
- Labour MP Josh Simons announced his resignation triggering the Makerfield by-election to allow Andy Burnham to return to parliament and stand for the Labour leadership.

😴 Sleep and wearables
- A Nanjing Sport University study published in 2026 in Frontiers in Physiology found high training loads led to less deep sleep and REM sleep, plus lower HRV.
- Whoop now has 2.5 million subscribers and a valuation exceeding 10 billion dollars after the Fitbit Air directly challenged its subscription model.
- SOND's Dreambuds startup, led by Bose's former head of sleep Yadid Ayzenberg, raised 7 million dollars and captures 12 physiological signals to help users improve sleep in real time.

📈 B2B SaaS and go-to-market
- Exaforce, an AI-native security operations platform, raised a 125 million dollar Series B on May 12 to advance its real-time security knowledge graph and expand go-to-market in Japan and Europe.
- Nearly half, 46 percent, of US-based B2B go-to-market leaders plan to increase their AI sales tool investment in 2026, signalling major GTM strategy shifts toward agentic platforms and conversation intelligence.
- Top-quartile SaaS operators have held their CAC payback at under 12 months, a level Bessemer Venture Partners flags as the line between efficient and structurally inefficient go-to-market motion.

Today's question: With 46 percent of B2B GTM leaders planning to increase AI sales tool investment this year, are you positioning your fractional advisory clients to be ahead of that curve, or are you still seeing them treat agentic platforms as optional rather than foundational to their 2026 go-to-market motion?
